summary: Lead replaced stalled historical_researcher writer for M6-SCENARIO-DATA-PIPELINE-001. Original writer 019f07c1-17ed-7300-a467-cb7062d5064f was closed after repeated wait timeouts and no REVIEW/BLOCKED handoff; replacement writer 019f07d2-14c6-7542-a409-5bf97fdcf352 is assigned to continue existing uncommitted allowed-path work and return a REVIEW handoff.

tests_run.result: Timed out across repeated waits without REVIEW/BLOCKED handoff.
Submitted status request 019f07cd-1d0f-7892-80fb-7f3098adbb2e; no final handoff returned before replacement.
Spawned replacement historical_researcher writer 019f07d2-14c6-7542-a409-5bf97fdcf352, nickname Estuary.

decisions: Preserve existing uncommitted allowed-path implementation from original writer for replacement continuation.
Replacement writer must focus on completing acceptance coverage, required tests, and structured REVIEW handoff.
Do not touch unrelated untracked ui-samples/.
risks: Original writer produced partial implementation without handoff; replacement must audit it before continuing.
Content compatibility tests may still be missing and must be added before acceptance.
requested_action: Audit existing uncommitted allowed-path implementation, complete M6-SCENARIO-DATA-PIPELINE-001, run required tests, and return historical_researcher REVIEW handoff for qa_reviewer.

acceptance_criteria: Three Alpha scenario data sets compile through explicit schemas with stable IDs, source or claim labels, dependency ordering, and deterministic manifest output.
Scenario records distinguish HISTORICAL, INFERRED, COMPOSITE, and FICTIONAL material and reject unlabeled or unsourced formal claims.
The pipeline validates references needed by diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, map candidates, policy, events, encyclopedia entries, and start-to-victory fixtures without implementing those systems.
No production art, audio, branding, cultural symbol lock, commercial release artifact, telemetry, server behavior, or arbitrary-code mod support is added.
required_tests: Content compatibility tests cover bad references, missing labels, unstable IDs, duplicate scenario keys, and deterministic manifest output.
